_template_notes: This is the canonical template for .estudy files.
It contains exactly one example of every valid chapter element type and subtype.
Subtype names use the current vocabulary (post-April-2026 rename).
Delete the '_comment' fields before use — they are documentation only.
Delete or replace the '_template_notes' and all '_comment' keys before publishing.

elements.text: This is the chapter introduction. It sets the scene before the first Bible question. It should be engaging and draw the reader into the chapter theme. It can contain bold or italic text.

elements.passageText: <sup>16</sup>For this is the way God loved the world: he gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life.

elements.question: What does this verse tell us about God's motivation in sending his Son?

elements.text: This is a bridge paragraph. It connects two sections of questions, provides context, or develops a theme. It can span multiple sentences and contains bold or italic text as needed.

elements.question: What do you understand by the word grace? Think about it before tapping 'Find out'.

elements.caption: An optional caption shown below the image.

elements.instruction: Choose the option that best reflects how well you agree or disagree with each statement.
statements: I have a clear understanding of what I believe.
I am currently growing in my faith.
I regularly spend time with God in prayer and Bible reading.

elements.question: What is one thing from this chapter you want to take away and act on this week?

elements.text: This is the chapter closing summary. It draws together the key themes of the chapter and points forward. It should be encouraging and leave the reader with something clear to hold on to.

qaCallouts.question: What do you understand by the word grace? Think about it before tapping 'Find out'.
qaCallouts.answer:
Grace is often understood as God being lenient or overlooking our faults — a kind of divine tolerance. But the Bible means something more specific and more costly.

Grace is God giving us what we do not deserve, at great cost to himself. It is not God lowering his standards; it is God meeting his own standards on our behalf, through Christ.

studyOnboardingSlides.heading: Study Title
studyOnboardingSlides.body:
A short description of the study — what it covers and who it is for.

Work through each chapter at your own pace, or use it with a group.

studyOnboardingSlides.heading: Read, reflect, write
studyOnboardingSlides.body: Each chapter has Bible questions tied to specific passages, and personal reflection questions at the end. Tap ↗ beside any Bible reference to read the passage without leaving the app.

studyOnboardingSlides.heading: Saved on your device
studyOnboardingSlides.body: Your answers are saved automatically as you type, and stored on this device. Use the Share button at the bottom of each chapter to send your answers to a group leader or mentor.

studyOnboardingSlides.heading: Adjust to your preference
studyOnboardingSlides.body: If you need bigger text, go straight to Settings now.
studyOnboardingSlides.bodyAfter: Tap the ⚙ icon in the top corner to open Settings any time to change text size or switch to dark mode. Tap Contents ☰ to jump to any chapter.

studyOnboardingSlides.heading: How to use this study
studyOnboardingSlides.body: If you want a full guide to getting the most from this study — whether on your own or in a group — tap the button below.
studyOnboardingSlides.bodyAfter: You can also find it any time via the Contents ☰ menu.

licence.name: Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International
freedoms: Share: copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format
Adapt: remix, transform, and build upon the material for any purpose, even commercially
conditions: Attribution: you must give appropriate credit, provide a link to the licence, and indicate if changes were made
ShareAlike: if you remix, transform, or build upon the material, you must distribute your contributions under the same licence as the original
